extraction.one_line_summary: KÄMNA Lennard won from a 15-rider breakaway after mass GC carnage saw Kruijswijk, Buchmann, and Mühlberger crash out plus Roglic's early crash
tactical_patterns: Jumbo visibly vulnerable without Kruijswijk: Roglic survived only because rivals couldn't exploit his crash damage on final climb
Landa attacked late on Megeve runway but lacked finishing power, revealing his form peak still insufficient despite shedding weaker climbers
15-rider breakaway given 4+ minutes indicates peloton chaos from crashes disrupted normal chase dynamics throughout stage

rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: first WorldTour win at 23 shows Bora found a stage-hunting breakthrough climber post-lockdown
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: crashed at km 82 and barely defended on runway despite no attacks, saved only by rivals' passivity
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: negative: dislocated shoulder ends Dauphine, compromises Tour selection for Jumbo's co-leadership plan
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: negative: crashed out while 3rd overall, exposing Bora's Tour depth crisis after losing three riders in one stage
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: dropped early and sweating profusely signals form far from Tour-ready despite stage race opportunity
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: lost 26:30 confirms he's riding into form rather than competing, selection for Tour now questionable
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: initiated late acceleration on runway but couldn't sustain, showing tactical ambition exceeds current diesel
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: attacked for 3rd place after struggling previous day, signals rapid form recovery pattern pre-Tour
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: moved to 2nd overall and tested Roglic on runway, confirming GC threat status after injury doubts
